narrative.headline: What 2019 Jeep Wrangler owners report most
narrative.summary: Steering complaints dominate owner reports for this model year, with many describing an uncontrollable shaking or wobbling while cornering or driving over bumps — a symptom owners have termed "death wobble." Electrical system and suspension issues make up the next largest share of complaints, followed by powertrain incidents that include engine problems and, in some cases, vehicle fires. Across all areas, a recurring theme is that affected vehicles were taken out of service at dealerships for extended periods.

issues.title: Steering
issues.blurb: Owners report the vehicle shaking uncontrollably when cornering or going over bumps, a condition some owners refer to as 'death wobble,' which they describe as making the vehicle undrivable. · 3 crash · 1 injured

issues.sample: PUSH BUTTON START WITH KEYLESS ENTRY.  VEHICLE DOES NOT GIVE AUDIBLE WARNING WHEN YOU LEAVE THE VEHICLE RUNNING ON EXIT.   THIS IS A SAFETY HAZARD AND COULD CAUSE DEATH IF PARKED IN YOUR HOME GARAGE.  IT IS VERY EASY PUT THE GEARSHIFT IN PARK AND EXIT THE VEHICLE WITHOUT TURNING
issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: Owners report that keyless push-button start vehicles continue running after the driver exits without providing an audible warning, which owners flag as a potential carbon monoxide hazard in enclosed spaces such as a home garage. · 3 fire · 3 crash · 2 injured

issues.title: Suspension
issues.blurb: Owners report uncontrollable shaking and vibration when driving over bumps or around corners, consistent with the steering-related 'death wobble' symptom, leaving the vehicle undrivable and requiring dealer service.

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: Owners report engine revving, difficulty shifting, loud banging sounds, and in some cases smoke and fire that fully engulfed the vehicle while driving. · 4 fire · 1 crash
